title: Dortmanna subdentata
taxonomy: Dortmanna subdentata is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It belongs to the genus Dortmanna, which is a small group of plants that are related to the myrtle family. Within the Myrtaceae family, Dortmanna subdentata is classified as part of the order Myrtales. The plant's classification at the kingdom level is Plantae, and it is a member of the clade Angiosperms. At the highest taxonomic level, Dortmanna subdentata is classified as a eudicot.

intro: Dortmanna subdentata, also known as Indian gentian or gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae, characterized by its vibrant yellow flowers and distinctive growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including its ability to stimulate digestion, improve appetite, and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Dortmanna subdentata include gentiopicrin, gentianine, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
